free_text: Measurement of the Pu-239 and U-235 fission
cross-sections and their ratio for neutron
DATA of Tables 5,6,7 are presented in SUBENTs
Method of the slowing-down-time in lead
Two ionization chambers with layers of Pu-239
(20 mg) and U-235 (40 mg) on aluminum cylinders of
30mm diameter and 45 mm length. Filled with mixture of
Argon and 50% methane under pressure 2 Atm (2.E+5 Pa)
Boron counters: SNM-20 in lead cube, SNM-3 in
graphite prism. Used for measurement of neutron beam
Corrected by Westcott factors(1.052 for Pu-239,
0.977 for U-235) values of c-s, used for normalization.
Error due to background neutrons. Maximal
Error due to increasing of B-10(n,alpha)
Error due to detectors time resolution
Errors were added in COMMON and DATA blocks.

from 1/v low, 4% in 15 - 30 keV neutron energy range,
